title: No clear upgrade path for WSL
body:
Hi,
I use Podman on my Windows installation.
I've noticed the WSL2 OS had Fedora 40 and I tried to upgrade via the regular steps here:
https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/quick-docs/upgrading-fedora-offline/
However this didn't work and broke the setup.

The WSL2 machine needs specific configuration in my setup, namely `resolv.conf`, so it is frustrating having to wipe and recreate the WSL2 VM.

I believe it would be great to either or both:
1. Document how to upgrade the OS in the WSL2 VM
2. Create an automatic upgrade process that can be triggered via e.g. `podman machine upgrade`

Please let me know how I can contribute to this.

Bests,
Giovanni

comments.body:
This is something we are working on.  Everything but WSL has a clear path moving forward but because of how WSL is constructed, this is proving to be difficult.

Have you considered exploring how to customize your machines performing an init?  there are some options that might be helpful to you depending on what all requires customization.

@baude does `machine init` support that?
Because namely what I need to achieve is:

1. Add the proper block to avoid resolv.conf symlink in `/etc/wsl.conf`
2. Replace `/etc/resolv.conf` with a manual one

I know I can customize CPUs and RAM, not config files though.

@ItachiSan I can think of a couple of ways to do this.

1. Pull our tarball, open it, change stuff, tar it.  Then feed it to `init`.
2. Use a simple ansible playbook that gets executed after the machine is created.  We added a `--playbook` option several releases ago which makes this super easy.  If you do this, remember that it takes a little bit of time for the playbook to be run when first starting, so there is a small gap of time there.

I would favor the second of the two options, in part bc if you have an issue with the first, we are going to tell you we cant help because it is a custom image.  

I hope this helps and would love to hear what you end up doing.  If everything works out, let us know and someone will close the issue if appropriate.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I managed for now to avoid network issues via the usermode network option.

This still does not solve the WSL VM upgrade though...

I am afraid that the only way to update the machine image on WSL today is to remove the machine and recreate a new one. 

The problem is that the machine data gets lost in the process. You can backup and restore images and containers with `podman image save` / `podman image load` and `podman container export` / `podman container import`, but it gets complicated if you need metadata and configs. It's a complete migration to a new machine, and we don't have any guide or command to help with that.
Hello, that is exactly my point. Not sure what can be done to improve the current situation.
